bead.title: auto-route: cost-aware tier scoring picks cheapest tier meeting power_hint_fit
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Once per-tier candidates are enumerated under a multi-tier subscription harness, the default scoring must select the cheapest tier that meets the power_hint_fit for the prompt. Downstream evidence: a 2207-token implementation prompt at role=implementer scored opus 100.5 (the lone claude candidate) under current behavior; the same prompt with sonnet-4.6 pinned scored 196.0. With both tiers enumerated by the sibling fix, default weights should produce sonnet>opus on impl-band prompts because cost_input_per_m (3 vs 15) and cost_output_per_m (15 vs 75) dominate.

ROOT CAUSE (pointers; confirm during fix)
- This child depends on per-tier enumeration landing (sibling child enumerate-per-tier-candidates). Once both claude/opus-4.7 and claude/sonnet-4.6 reach the scorer, score_components in the routing_decision event should show cost dominance for sonnet at impl power band. Verify scoring weights at internal/routing scorer (the same package that emits power_hint_fit / score_components events).
- Sonnet's power=8 is sufficient for role=implementer at ~2000-3000 tokens; opus's power=10 is not required to clear the hint. The fix here is to confirm the existing weights produce sonnet>opus given correct enumeration, and to add a regression test pinning that behavior; if weights need tuning to satisfy the AC, scope the tune narrowly.

PROPOSED FIX (direction)
Deterministic scoring test that constructs a routing.Inputs for a 2000-3000-token implementation prompt at role=implementer with both claude/opus-4.7 and claude/sonnet-4.6 in the candidate pool and asserts the chosen tier is sonnet-4.6. Only adjust scoring weights if the assertion fails with current weights — and only by the minimum needed.

NON-SCOPE
- Candidate enumeration mechanics (covered by sibling child enumerate-per-tier-candidates; this child assumes it lands first).
- --model pin routing destination (sibling child model-pin-subscription-route).
- filter_reason emission for excluded tiers (sibling child explicit-filter-reason).
- Re-tuning scoring across other roles/power bands beyond what is required to satisfy the AC.
- openrouter 401, DDx-side changes, catalog metadata.

CROSS-REF
https://github.com/easel/fizeau/issues/6
bead.acceptance:
1. TestSonnetBeatsOpusForImplementerPrompt — given both claude/opus-4.7 and claude/sonnet-4.6 in the candidate pool, a routing decision for a 2000-3000-token prompt with role=implementer under policy=default selects claude/sonnet-4.6 as the winner. The assertion reads the chosen tier directly from routing_decision and is deterministic (no flake).
2. TestImplementerScoreComponentsCostDominates — score_components for the same scenario shows the cost dimension as the deciding factor (sonnet's lower cost_input_per_m and cost_output_per_m produce a higher composite score than opus despite opus's higher power).
3. Depends-on: sibling child enumerate-per-tier-candidates must land first; this child's tests assume per-tier enumeration is in place.
4. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./... green.
5. Existing scoring/routing tests pass: service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go, internal/routing tests.
6. lefthook run pre-commit green.
